subject: geese
one_liner: Daily five-minute honk lessons that get your flock conversational in eleven regional dialects before the fall migration.
brand_voice_notes: Dry, faux-corporate flight-operations register. Treats goose bilingualism as a solved procurement problem. Streak enforcement is framed as customer success. Menace is delivered in the passive voice. Never winks.

hero.h1: Your flock leaves in October. Their honk isn't ready.
hero.sub: Skein teaches migratory geese conversational honk in eleven regional flyway dialects, five minutes a day, so nobody lands in Ontario sounding like New Jersey.

features.title: Dialect placement
features.blurb: A four-minute assessment honk places each goose into one of eleven flyway dialects, from Upper Chesapeake Estuary to LaGuardia Terminal B. Placement is final. Appeals are heard annually, in person, in Winnipeg.

features.title: Streak enforcement
features.blurb: Streaks do not pause for migration. Skein's satellite relay confirms lesson completion at cruising altitude, weather permitting. Weather has never been the problem.

features.title: Hiss de-escalation track
features.blurb: An elective module that teaches geese to express territorial rage in complete, grammatically correct sentences. Enrollment remains at zero across all markets. We are patient.

pricing_tiers.name: Gosling
pricing_tiers.tagline: For geese who are not serious.

pricing_tiers.name: Migratory
pricing_tiers.tagline: Billed annually. No refunds after departure.
perks: Streak protection through one named storm system

pricing_tiers.name: Full Skein
pricing_tiers.tagline: For flocks of up to 40. The V is a team.

testimonials.quote: My gander tested into Upper Chesapeake in six weeks. He hisses in the subjunctive now. We don't know how to feel about it.
testimonials.name: Diane Krolik
testimonials.title: Volunteer Waterfowl Liaison, Rockford Municipal Golf Course

testimonials.quote: We enrolled 300 geese ahead of the fall migration. Retention has been flawless. They have not left. They show no intention of leaving.
testimonials.name: Marcus Ehle
testimonials.title: Director of Airfield Wildlife Operations, Gerald R. Ford International Airport

testimonials.quote: The streak reminders are relentless and the geese know it. Lesson compliance at my pond is at 96 percent. Morale is unclear.
testimonials.name: Priya Vaidyanathan
testimonials.title: Assistant Superintendent of Ponds, City of Naperville Park District, District 4

demo_screen_concept: A Duolingo-style lesson screen for Unit 4: Tarmac & Terminal dialect, styled as a flight-operations console. A fake honk-transmission player with a static waveform, a 'translate this honk' exercise with four written multiple-choice transcriptions, three hearts, a 47-day streak counter, a per-unit XP bar, and a compliance-desk notification banner that escalates in politeness-inverted order with each wrong answer. Answers actually grade: hearts decrement on misses, streak and XP tick up on hits, and running out of hearts grounds the lesson.

images.rationale: Hero skipped: Swiss-modernist timetable direction is typographic, and the hero's right column is a split-flap departures board built in HTML. Demo assets skipped: the demo is a lesson console (multiple-choice text, waveform, telemetry), not photo-shaped. Full budget went to three deadpan goose corporate headshots for the human-named testimonials, one per quote.

items.prompt: Deadpan corporate editorial headshot photograph of a single adult Canada goose posed like a professional staff-directory portrait, head and upper neck in slight three-quarter view, composed and confident, soft studio key light, seamless cool porcelain-grey studio backdrop with a faint cobalt-blue undertone (#F2F4F7 with hints of #113FB4), crisp feather detail, shallow depth of field, sober corporate-aviation mood. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Deadpan corporate editorial headshot photograph of a single stern adult Canada goose photographed like an airport operations director's ID portrait, head-on frontal pose, unimpressed authoritative expression, even studio lighting, seamless desaturated muted cobalt-blue studio backdrop (a greyed-down #113FB4), crisp feather detail, formal institutional mood. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Deadpan corporate editorial headshot photograph of a single alert adult Canada goose posed like a municipal parks administrator's annual-report portrait, slight left three-quarter view with neck upright and attentive, neutral polite expression, soft window-style studio light, seamless light slate-grey studio backdrop with a cool blue-grey cast (#68758A lightened), crisp feather detail, earnest civic-administration mood. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

design_direction.style: Swiss-modernist flight-operations timetable, porcelain daylight mode. The page is staged like the operations brand of a mid-size national airline that has been aimed, with total sincerity, at waterfowl. A cool porcelain-grey ground carries a strict modernist column grid with hairline rules, flush-left ragged-right type, and generous functional whitespace; panels are flat, borderless or 1px-ruled, with no shadows, no gradients, no rounded clay, no glass. One aviation cobalt carries the wordmark, headlines, structural rules, links, and all brand ink; one rationed safety orange is reserved strictly for the single primary CTA, the live 'boarding' status chip, and anything that constitutes a compliance warning, and appears almost nowhere else. Barlow Condensed sets the wordmark and headlines in tall uppercase signage caps with tight tracking, like gate typography; Libre Franklin carries plain, procedural body copy. Every figure that pretends to be operational telemetry (streak days, dialect counts, departure windows, tier prices, compliance percentages) is set in Space Mono with tabular figures so it reads like a departure board, not a marketing number. The hero's right column is a split-flap departures board listing flyway routes with dialect requirements and statuses. Motion is board-native: split-flap rows tick in on load with a small stagger, status chips flip between states, the CTA translates down 1px on press, and everything is gated behind prefers-reduced-motion. No dark mode, no neon, no paper grain, no hard offset shadows, no editorial serifs. Reads like a heavily certified aviation-training vendor that considers goose bilingualism a routine regulatory matter.

fonts.body: 'Libre Franklin', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

design_direction.archetype: Language-learning app landing staged as an airline operations brand: split hero with a departures-board panel of flyway routes, features as numbered pre-flight checks, three crew-report testimonials, pricing as fare classes, single safety-orange CTA, and a demo handoff to the Unit 4 lesson console.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Split-flap row ticks with 60ms stagger on load, status-chip flips between DELAYED and BOARDING, hairline rules draw in on scroll, CTA translates down 1px on press; all gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Two-column modernist grid: left column carries the Barlow Condensed uppercase headline, procedural sub, and the single orange CTA; right column is a flat cobalt departures-board panel in Space Mono listing flyway routes (MISSISSIPPI FLYWAY, ATLANTIC FLYWAY, LAGUARDIA T-B) with required dialect and status chips. A hairline-ruled telemetry strip under the hero shows deadpan stats: geese enrolled, dialects, average streak.
